revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing a bunch of english language kofun articles

fixing error that broke all the categories

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular burial mound. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3303.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular burial mound. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[Grave goods]] included [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[Mineyama Station]] on the [[Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103960471]] Wikidata name: Ubusunayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>

It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103960471]] Wikidata name: Ubusunayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103960471]] Wikidata name: Ubusunayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3303.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.

The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103960471]] Wikidata name: Ubusunayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from [[:en:Mineyama Station|Mineyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kyoto Tango Railway|Kyoto Tango Railway]] [[:en:Miyazu Line|Miyazu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103960471]] Wikidata name: Ubusunayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from {{ill|Mineyama Station|en|Mineyama Station|WD=Q6864579|lt=Mineyama Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|WD=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3303.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[en:Ubusunayama Kofun]][[ja:産土山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from {{ill|Mineyama Station|en|Mineyama Station|WD=Q6864579|lt=Mineyama Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|WD=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline}} *[https://www.city.kyotango.lg.jp/top/soshiki/kyoikuiinkai/bunkazaihogo/3/1/2/3303.html Kyōtango City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ubusunayama Kofun | native_name =産土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ubusunayama kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ubusunayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.74144444444445|N|135.11194444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotango|WD=Q513897|1=Kyōtango}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|44|29.2|N|135|06|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Ubusunayama Kofun'''|産土山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Tangochomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtango, Kyoto|Kyōtango, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202076 |title=産土山古墳 |trans-title= Ubusunayama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Ubusunayama Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun. It is located on the saddle of a hill at the mouth of the Takeno River, in the center of the west coast of the [[:en:Tango Peninsula|Tango Peninsula]] overlooking the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It dates from the middle of the Kofun period, or the middle of the 5th century. It was first excavated in 1939, during which time a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} sarcophagus was discovered directly buried in the upper part of the central part of the mound with the main axis in the east-west direction. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads and round glass beads, iron swords with ring or deer horn hilts, and wooden bows. The northern half of the mound collapsed due to flood damage in 1953. For this reason and due to legal issues with property ownership, when the National Historic Site designation was granted in 1957, only the portion of the tumulus located within the grounds of the neighboring {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} were designated, and the portion on private land on the western side of the tumulus was not. After a more extensive archaeological survey was conducted from 1995 to 1996, the true size of the tumulus was determined to be 54 meters in diameter. In December 1998, the borders of the National Historic Site designation were modified to include some finds from this survey, and to exclude a portion of the area outside the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> Cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were also excavated.
The site is about a 20-minute drive from {{ill|Mineyama Station|en|Mineyama Station|WD=Q6864579|lt=Mineyama Station|1=Mineyama Station}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyazu Line|en|Miyazu Line|WD=Q6884480|lt=Miyazu Line|1=Miyazu Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Ubusunayamakofun (1).jpg|Descriptive placard Ubusunayamakofun (2).jpg|National Historic Site monument </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
